tools.name: compose_parts_basket
tools.description: Compose a COMPLETE, brand- and size-compatible purchase list (inkooplijst) for a physical job in the Netherlands - every fitting, adapter, end cap and small part professionals usually forget. Asks up to 3 clarifying questions when a critical spec (diameter, brand, amperage, substrate) is unknown; pa

tools.name: report_order_exception
tools.description: Report a return (retour), delivery shortage (tekort) or damage (schade) on a coordinated order. AstraNL takes over coordination with the merchant under the merchant's own return/delivery terms; escrow stays HELD until resolution is confirmed. Zero routine for merchant and client.

tools.name: create_task
tools.description: Create a real-world physical-services task on AstraNL — a coordination protocol for the Netherlands (KvK 88449335). AstraNL matches the task to verified providers and returns a task_id. Use when: the user needs a physical-world job done in NL (painting, cleaning, plumbing, roofing, electrical, garde

tools.name: check_task
tools.description: Look up the current status of a previously created AstraNL task by its numeric task_id. Use when: a user asks 'what happened with task #N', after create_task to confirm matching, or when polling for status changes. DO NOT use for: enumerating all tasks (no listing endpoint is exposed via MCP); check

tools.name: search_robots
tools.description: Search the AstraNL Robot Index for robots capable of performing a given task type. The index covers 46+ commercial and pilot-stage robots across 11 categories: humanoid industrial/consumer, cobots, industrial arms, AMRs, quadrupeds, service bots, commercial drones, agricultural drones. Use when: com

tools.name: estimate_cost
tools.description: Estimate the cost of a task in EUR based on AstraNL's historical pricing data, with a side-by-side comparison of human-provider vs robot-automation cost. Use when: a user is exploring whether a task is feasible within their budget; before calling create_task to set realistic expectations; comparing

tools.name: search
tools.description: Search AstraNL coordination data (robots registry, service categories, and merchant parts by keyword). Returns a list of results, each with an id and a short title/snippet, for use with the fetch tool. Read-only. This is the generic discovery entry point expected by agent frameworks.

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Fetch the full record for a single result id returned by search (robot passport, category detail, or merchant SKU). Read-only.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "create_task" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Create a real-world physical-services task on AstraNL — a coordination protocol for the Netherlands (KvK 88449335). AstraNL matches the task to verified provide…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "estimate_cost" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Estimate the cost of a task in EUR based on AstraNL's historical pricing data, with a side-by-side comparison of human-provider vs robot-automation cost. Use wh…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "streamable" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: compose_parts_basket, create_task · Private data: create_task, fetch · Public sink: create_task.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: compose_parts_basket, create_task · Private data: create_task, fetch · Public sink: create_task

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
